description: 交互精准度提升与高级选择系统：彻底修复反向拖拽绘制和缩放锚点偏移的致命 Bug，引入鼠标框选（Marquee Selection）与对象组合（Grouping）功能。

userStories.title: Fix reverse drag drawing failure with coordinate normalization
userStories.description: As a user, I want to draw shapes by dragging in any direction (including left/up), so that shapes never disappear or distort when I drag backwards.
acceptanceCriteria: For closed shapes (Rectangle, Rhombus): pointermove and pointerup both execute coordinate normalization: x = Math.min(startX, currentX), y = Math.min(startY, currentY), width = Math.abs(currentX - startX), height = Math.abs(currentY - startY)

For vector shapes (Line, Arrow, Freehand): DO NOT apply strict top-left normalization. Preserve original start and end direction points (e.g., x1, y1 to x2, y2) so arrows point correctly regardless of drag direction
Rectangle drawn from (500,500) to (200,200) leaves a perfect rectangle on canvas
SceneElement width and height for closed shapes are never negative numbers
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Fix resize handle offset with absolute coordinate calculation
userStories.description: As a user, I want shape edges to follow my cursor precisely when resizing via handles, so that I can accurately adjust shape dimensions.
acceptanceCriteria: Resize uses absolute coordinate calculation method, no width += deltaX relative accumulation
Dragging se (bottom-right) handle: top-left corner (x, y) is completely locked, new width = mouseX - originX, new height = mouseY - originY
Dragging nw (top-left) handle: bottom-right corner is locked, x, y, width, height recalculated from opposite corner
All 8 handles (nw, ne, sw, se, n, s, e, w) correctly lock the opposite anchor and recalculate dimensions
Mouse screen coordinates correctly converted to world coordinates (accounting for panX, panY, zoom)
Minimum width and height constrained to 5px, preventing axis-crossing flip
Dragging a rectangle's bottom-right handle outward then inward, the top-left corner stays completely stationary
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement marquee selection for batch-selecting shapes
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag a selection rectangle on empty canvas to batch-select multiple shapes, so I can efficiently work with many elements at once.
acceptanceCriteria: Marquee selection only active in Selection Tool mode
pointerdown on empty canvas (no shape hit) triggers marquee selection mode
pointermove draws semi-transparent blue rectangle on Interactive Canvas: fill rgba(0, 120, 255, 0.1) + blue dashed border
Marquee rectangle uses coordinate normalization (Math.min, Math.abs) to ensure positive width/height
pointerup calculates marquee bounding box and intersects with all non-deleted scene elements
Elements whose bounding box intersects or is fully contained within marquee rectangle are added to selectedElementIds
Interactive Canvas marquee rectangle cleared after pointerup
Static Canvas redraws to show selection highlight on newly selected elements
Three separate shapes on canvas, marquee covering part of two shapes selects both on release
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add groupIds array to SceneElement interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to add a groupIds array to the SceneElement interface so that shapes can be grouped and ungrouped.
acceptanceCriteria: SceneElement interface has new groupIds: string[] property (default empty array)
Existing element creation logic initializes groupIds: []
WebSocket sync and persistence correctly serialize/deserialize groupIds field
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement group and ungroup core logic
userStories.description: As a user, I want to group selected shapes together so that selecting one element selects the entire group, and I can move them as a unit.
acceptanceCriteria: Group: generates unique groupId (crypto.randomUUID()), pushes it into groupIds array of all selected elements
Ungroup: when only one group is selected, removes the groupId from all elements in that group
Clicking any element within a group automatically finds and selects all elements with the same groupId
Group selection highlight uses max bounding box with single dashed border
Dragging a group moves all elements within it by the same delta, preserving relative positions
CRITICAL: Disable resize handles for Group selections in this version to prevent complex scaling distortion. Groups can only be moved, not resized.
Both Group and Ungroup actions MUST push a snapshot to the history stack for Undo/Redo
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Group/Ungroup button to toolbar with state machine
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Group button in the toolbar that intelligently enables/disables based on my selection, so I can group and ungroup shapes with a single click.
acceptanceCriteria: New Group button added at rightmost position of toolbar first row (to the right of round corner button)
Button uses group SVG icon (merged square with dots at four corners)
Disabled state (grayed out) when selected element count < 2
Enabled state (normal) when selected element count >= 2 and elements are not in the same group, clicking groups them
Ungroup state when only one group object is selected, clicking ungroups it
Button state updates reactively when selectedElementIds changes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
